toolheads.name: A4T
toolheads.description: Lightweight toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, accepts standalone extruders.
toolheads.title: A4T

_new_item_sources.Clockwork2: | :information_source: **Clockwork2 carriage/Tap users**: Don't forget the 2x M3x8 SHCS that go behind the adapter | |
_new_item_sources.NF-Crazy Volcano:
| 1 | Hotend | Recommended hotend:
* **Dragon HF** with **Triangle Lab ZS-MZE-HF** (trianglelab.net)
Alternatives:
* Chube Compact
* Dragon UHF or UHF-Mini
* Dragon Ace `use the included 3mm spacer`
* Dragon Ace with MZE `use the included 3mm spacer`
* Rapido HF or UHF
* NF-Crazy Volcano `May melt plastic behind status LED without tape insulation`
* Revo-Voron
* Bambulab
* TZ-V6-2.0

`volcano blocks with oversize heater cartridges may require some insulation tape (aluminium/reflective) on the back of the LED housing that faces the hotend.` |
_new_item_sources.Cartographer: A printer that fits either Xol carriage, or a standard voron CW2 or Tap carriage. `NOT cartographer CNC carriage (see warning below)`
_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::2510:
| Lock the 2510 HE fan in place by sliding the HE fan duct up until the flexture locks.
`⚠️ Careful, don't pinch any wires`| |

toolheads.name: Crown Cooler
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, lightweight, features support for the Stealthburner X carriage, CPAP, standalone extruders, and UHF hotends.
toolheads.title: Crown Cooler

_new_item_sources.Vz HextrudORT: After a lot of experimenting with other cooling designs such as the [Tentacool](https://github.com/MirageC79/HextrudORT/tree/main/files/COOLING/TENTACOOL), I plan on merging the ideas produced from the V2.5 cooler, with the ideas from the Tentacool. I don't want to give a release date as it's a slow process, but a release is on the hoizon.

_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::CPAP: I intend to make some modified versions that have, improved CAD, a timeline (DK is a menance), Helicoil support, Regular BMG Support, PEEK tube lined filament path, 15/18mm CPAP tubing

toolheads.name: Dragon Burner
toolheads.description: Lightweight toolhead for Printers for Ants.
toolheads.title: Dragon Burner

_new_item_sources.Rapido UHF: **NOTE**: The [Rapid Burner](https://github.com/chirpy2605/voron/tree/main/V0/Rapid_Burner) toolhead supports the Rapido UHF and Dragon UHF hotends.
_new_item_sources.Dragon UHF: **NOTE**: The [Rapid Burner](https://github.com/chirpy2605/voron/tree/main/V0/Rapid_Burner) toolhead supports the Rapido UHF and Dragon UHF hotends.
_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::3010: This toolhead improves the cooling of the hotend and provides much improved part cooling when printing filament that needs it (e.g. PLA). Designed to use 2 4010 blower fans for part cooling and a 3010 fan for hotend cooling.

_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::CPAP: Fibre Steam is a robust and high performance CPAP toolhead designed for the Monolith Gantry. Fibre Steam's variants integrate the extruder into the toolhead which allows for accurate CoM balancing for the toolhead. CoM and the overall stiffness of your toolhead is integral to achieving good input shapers for high performance printing.

toolheads.name: Gustav
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Monolith gantry, features support for CPAP, UHF hotends, and beacon probe.
toolheads.title: Gustav

toolheads.name: Jabberwocky
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, features support for filament changers.
toolheads.title: Jabberwocky

_new_item_sources.Revo: - Basic Jabberwocky Kit - This kit allows for self sourcing your own toolhead and tool-board. Great for users that might have the components. It gives flexibility for open development or advanced users who want to use different toolheads and different electronics. Examples like UHF Rapido or Revo toolheads are being actively developed/tested. Visit the discord channel to find out what third-party or independent users have created for the Basic Jabberwocky Kits.

toolheads.name: Mailbox
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 0, features support for enhanced 5015 fan cooling.
toolheads.title: Mailbox

_new_item_sources.TAP: This toolhead should not lose any X or Z axis travel. There should be no Y axis loss on the v0.2, but on the v0.1 it may tap the door/tophat. Those with raised/square tophat/top frames should have no issues.

toolheads.name: Mantis
toolheads.description: Lightweight toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, accepts standalone extruders.
toolheads.title: Mantis

_new_item_sources.Unklicky: - The Klicky Probe carriage that was adapted was originally designed for the Dragon hotend. You'll need to use a taller probe body to reach the bed such as the one used by [Unklicky BFP](https://github.com/majarspeed/Unklicky). *This isn't an issue for Euclid or PCB Klicky*.
_new_item_sources.Klicky: - Support for JosAr's Klicky, VinnyCordeiro and WhoppingPochard's PCB Klicky, and Nionio6915's Euclid

toolheads.name: Mini-Stealthdort
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron V0, features support for standalone extruders.
toolheads.title: Mini-Stealthdort

toolheads.name: Rapid Burner
toolheads.description: Lightweight toolhead for Printers for Ants, variant of Dragonburner, features support for high flow hotends.
toolheads.title: Rapid Burner

_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::3010: Insert the fans. You will need to release the cable from the tabs on the front-end 3010 fan as well as the left part cooling 4010 fan. This is to allow the cable to be routed correctly. Care should be taken with the cables after doing this as too much movement could break off the wires from the fans. You can add cable relief with a dab of hot glue on the fan wire connections under the sticker.

toolheads.name: Reaper
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, inspired by Mantis.
toolheads.title: Reaper

_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::3010: ## Reaper uses a 3010 HE fan and dual 5015 part cooling fans.

toolheads.name: StovePipe
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 0, features CPAP support.
toolheads.title: StovePipe

_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::CPAP: # Stove Pipe - a CPAP fan implementation for the Voron v0.1
_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::5015: As a backup, you can also use a modified [MailBox Toolhead](https://github.com/waytotheweb/voron/blob/main/V0/5015_Toolhead) duct on the toolhead shroud and a 5015 fan! This can be swapped out without having to dismantle the toolhead.

toolheads.name: Takeoff
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, features an SLM design and large 3628 fans.
toolheads.title: Takeoff

_new_item_sources.Sherpa Micro: Sherpa Micro (With K-face), Mellow CNC Sherpa Micro supported, but no K-face is available
_new_item_sources.Chube: An SLM toolhead using 3628s and [Chube](https://chubehotend.com), with no X loss. There will be some Y loss (17mm~ from hitting the front door) unless you have a [clicky clacky door](https://github.com/tanaes/whopping_Voron_mods/tree/main/clickyclacky_door), a v2.4 that allows moving the bed back, or a 4040-based frame, such as DoomCube

toolheads.name: Yavoth
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 0 with support for HF and UHF hotends.
toolheads.title: Yavoth

_new_item_sources.hotend_fan::4010: This is a toolhead for High and Ultra High Flow style hotends using angled 4010 part cooling fans and a 2510 hotend fan.
_new_item_sources.hotend_fan::3010: It uses a 2510 hotend fan sighted at an advantageous height to offer improved hotend cooling performance compared to a 3010 hotend fan.
_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::2510: This is a toolhead for High and Ultra High Flow style hotends using angled 4010 part cooling fans and a 2510 hotend fan.
